name: MATHEMATICS
question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/4/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minutes time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains three sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Questions number 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

A bag contains 3 red and 4 white balls. Three balls are drawn at random, one-by-one without replacement from the bag. If the first ball drawn is red in colour, then find the probability that the remaining two balls drawn are also red in colour.

 2
2.

A coin is tossed twice. The following table shows the probability distribution of the number of tails:

X 0 1 2
P(X) K 6K 9K
  1. Find the value of K.
  2. Is the coin tossed biased or unbiased? Justify your answer.
 2
3.

The foot of a perpendicular drawn from the point (-2, -1, -3) on a plane is (1, -3, 3). Find the equation of the plane.

 2
4.(i)

If {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } {/tex} = 400 and {tex} | \vec { b } |{/tex} = 5, then find the value of {tex} | \vec { a } |{/tex}.

 2

OR
(ii)

Find all the possible vectors of magnitude {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} which are equally inclined to the coordinate axes.

 2
5.

Find the general solution of the differential equation sec2x tan y dx + sec2y tan x dy = 0

 2
6.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^1 x^2 e^x d x{/tex}

 2

SECTION B

Questions number 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Find the area of the region {(x, y) : {tex}\left.x^2 \leq y \leq x+2\right\}{/tex}, using integration.

 3
8.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex}

 3
9.

If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitude, then prove that the vector {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} is equally inclined to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex}. Also, find the angle between {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex}.

 3
10.(i)

If a line makes 60o and 45o angles with the positive directions of x-axis and z-axis respectively, then find the angle that it makes with the positive direction of y-axis. Hence, write the direction cosines of the line.

 3

OR
(ii)

Check whether the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}{/tex} = z are skew or not.

 3

SECTION C

Questions number 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the equations of the planes passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} = 6 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0, which are at a distance of 1 unit from the origin.

 4
12.(i)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}{/tex} = 0, given that y(1) = 0.

 4

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation x (y3 + x3) dy = (2y4 + 5x3y) dx.

 4
13.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(2 \log \cos x-\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex}

 4

Case-Study Based Question
14.

In a game of Archery, each ring of the Archery target is valued. The centremost ring is worth 10 points and rest of the rings are allotted points 9 to 1 in sequential order moving outwards.
Archer A is likely to earn 10 points with a probability of 0.8 and Archer B is likely the earn 10 points with a probability of 0.9.

If both of them hit the Archery target, then find the probability that
(i)

exactly one of them earns 10 points.

 2
(ii)

both of them earn 10 points.

 2

If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitude, then prove that the vector {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} is equally inclined to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} . Also, find the angle between {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} . 3 10. Hence, write the direction cosines of the line. 3 OR (ii) Check whether the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}{/tex} = z are skew or not. 3 SECTION C Questions number 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each. 11. Find the equations of the planes passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} = 6 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0, which are at a distance of 1 unit from the origin. 4 12. (i) Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}{/tex} = 0, given that y(1) = 0. 4 OR (ii) Find the general solution of the differential equation x (y 3 + x 3 ) dy = (2y 4 + 5x 3 y) dx. 4 13. Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(2 \log \cos x-\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex} 4 Case-Study Based Question 14. In a game of Archery, each ring of the Archery target is valued. The centremost ring is worth 10 points and rest of the rings are allotted points 9 to 1 in sequential order moving outwards. Archer A is likely to earn 10 points with a probability of 0.8 and Archer B is likely the earn 10 points with a probability of 0.9. If both of them hit the Archery target, then find the probability that (i) exactly one of them earns 10 points. 2 (ii) both of them earn 10 points. 2
answer_key_html:
Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/4/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minutes time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains three sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Questions number 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

A bag contains 3 red and 4 white balls. Three balls are drawn at random, one-by-one without replacement from the bag. If the first ball drawn is red in colour, then find the probability that the remaining two balls drawn are also red in colour.

Solution:

Total balls = 3 + 4 = 7
Now if 1st ball was red then red balls left = 2
Total balls = 6
P(B2) = {tex}\frac{{}^2C_1}{{}^6C_1}{/tex}​​
​​​​​And now for 3rd ball, red ball left = 1
Total balls= 5
P(B3) = {tex}\frac{{}^1C_1}{{}^5C_1}{/tex}
Now, required probability= {tex}\frac{{}^2C_1}{{}^6C_1}\times\frac{{}^1C_1}{{}^5C_1}=\frac26\times\frac15=\frac1{15}{/tex}

 2
2.

A coin is tossed twice. The following table shows the probability distribution of the number of tails:

X 0 1 2
P(X) K 6K 9K
  1. Find the value of K.
  2. Is the coin tossed biased or unbiased? Justify your answer.
Solution:
  1. We know that, the Total probability should be equal to 1 for sum of all possible events under the given experiment.
    Therefore,
    P(0) + P(1) + P(2) = 1
    K + 6K + 9K = 1 (From the table)
    16K = 1
    {tex}\because{/tex} K = {tex}\frac 1{16}{/tex}
  2. From results we can see that events are not equally likely,
    hence the coin tossed is unbiased,
    Because P(0) {tex}\ne{/tex} P(2)
    {tex}\because{/tex} K {tex}\ne{/tex} 9K.
 2
3.

The foot of a perpendicular drawn from the point (-2, -1, -3) on a plane is (1, -3, 3). Find the equation of the plane.

Solution:

We observe that the required plane passes through the point Q (1, - 3, 3) and is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow { P Q } = ( \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ) - ( - 2 \hat { i } - {/tex}{tex}\hat { j } - 3 \hat { k } ) = 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k }{/tex}. Thus, the required plane passes through the point Q having a position vector {tex}\vec { a } = \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} and is normal to {tex}\vec { n } = \overrightarrow { P Q } = 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k }{/tex}. So, the vector equation of the plane is

{tex}\vec { r } \cdot \vec { n } = \vec { a } \cdot \vec { n }{/tex}
or, {tex}\vec { r } \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ) = ( \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex}
or, {tex}\vec { r } \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex} = 3 + 6 + 18
or, {tex}\vec { r } \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex} = 27
The cartesian equation of the plane is {tex}( x \hat { i } + y \hat { j } + z \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex}= 27 or, 3x - 2y + 6z = 27

 2
4.(i)

If {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } {/tex} = 400 and {tex} | \vec { b } |{/tex} = 5, then find the value of {tex} | \vec { a } |{/tex}.

Solution:

Given, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} and {tex} | \vec { a } | = 5{/tex}
Consider, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 400{/tex}
{tex} [ \because | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \text { and } | \vec { a } . \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \cos \theta \|{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow d | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \left( \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta \right) = 400{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 25 | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400 \quad [ \because | \vec { a } | = 5 ]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | ^{ 2 } = 16 \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | = 4{/tex}
[{tex} \therefore{/tex} length cannot be negative]

 2

OR
(ii)

Find all the possible vectors of magnitude {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} which are equally inclined to the coordinate axes.

Solution:

Direction vector equally inclined to coordinate axis = {tex}\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex}
Vector = {tex}5\sqrt3\times\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex}
Vector = {tex}5\widehat i+5\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex}

 2
5.

Find the general solution of the differential equation sec2x tan y dx + sec2y tan x dy = 0

Solution:

Given Differential equation is, sec2x tan y dx + sec2y tan x dy = 0
Dividing by tan x tan y, we have
{tex}\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow\int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x} \right| + \log \left| {\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex}    ...{tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{{f'\left( x \right)}}{{f\left( x \right)}}} dx = \log \left| {f\left( x \right)} \right|} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \tan x\tan y = \pm c = C{/tex}    ... {tex}\left[ {\because \,\left| t \right| = a\left( {a \geqslant 0} \right) \Rightarrow t = \pm a} \right]{/tex}

 2
6.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^1 x^2 e^x d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int_0^1 x^2 e^x d x=\left[x^2 \cdot e^x\right]_0^1-\int_0^1 2 x \ e^x d x{/tex} {tex}=\left(1 \cdot e^1-0\right)-2 \int_0^1 x \ e^x dx{/tex}
= {tex}e-2\left(\left[x \ e^x\right]_0^1-\int_0^1 1 . e^x d x\right){/tex}
= {tex}e-2\left(1 . e^1-0\right)+2 \int_0^1 e^x d x{/tex} = {tex}e-2 e+2\left[e^x\right]_0^1{/tex}
= -e + 2(e1 - e0) = -e + 2 (e - 1) = e - 2

 2

SECTION B

Questions number 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

Find the area of the region {(x, y) : {tex}\left.x^2 \leq y \leq x+2\right\}{/tex}, using integration.

Solution:


{tex}\because{/tex} y = x + 2 and y = x2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x2 = x + 2
x2 - x - 2 = 0
x2 - 2x + x - 2 = 0
x(x - 2) + 1(x - 2) = 0
(x - 2)(x + 1) = 0
x = 2 and -1
y = 2 + 2 = 4 and y = -1 + 2 = 1
So C(-1, 1) and D (2, 4)
Now required area = area covered by line - area of parabola
Required area = {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^2(x+2)dx-\int_\limits{-1}^2x^2dx{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\frac{x^2}2+2x\right]_{-1}^2-\left[\frac{x^3}3\right]_{-1}^2{/tex}
={tex}\left[\frac42+4-\frac12+2\right]-\left[\frac83-\frac{\left(-1\right)}3\right]{/tex}
Required area = {tex}\frac{15}2-3=\frac92{/tex} sq. unit.

 3
8.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex}

Solution:

Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } } \right] } d x{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + e ^ { - x } \right] } d x{/tex} ...(i)
Let 1 + e-x = t then,
d(1 + e-x) = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - e-x dx = dt
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac { - d t } { e ^ { - x } }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}dx = - ex dt
Putting 1 + e-x = t and dx = - ex dt in equation (i), we get,
{tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \times t } \times - e ^ { x } d t{/tex}
{tex}= - \int \frac { d t } { t }{/tex}
= - log |t| + c
= - log |1 + e-x| + c
= - log |1 + e-x| + c

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex} ​​​​​
|3 - x| = -(3 - x) for 1 and 2
|3 - x| = (3 - x) for 3 and 4
So, {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx+\int_1^2-(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx-\int_1^2(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\left[\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^4-\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^2+\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_3^4{/tex}
{tex}I=\left[{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-{\textstyle\frac12}\right]-\left[6-{\textstyle\frac42}-3+{\textstyle\frac12}\right]+{/tex} {tex}\left[12-{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-9+{\textstyle\frac92}\right]{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{11}2{/tex}​​​​​​​

 3
9.

If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitude, then prove that the vector {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} is equally inclined to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex}. Also, find the angle between {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex}.

Solution:

Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=x{/tex}
​​Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow aand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}be {tex}\theta_1{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{\overrightarrow a.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex}
​​​​{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\overrightarrow a\overrightarrow{.b}+2\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x^2+0+0}{x.\sqrt{4x^2+x^2+x^2+0}}{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x}{\sqrt6\;x}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex} ...(i)​​​​​​​
Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow cand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}be ​​​​​​​{tex}\theta_2{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{\overrightarrow c.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{2\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow c\overrightarrow{.b}+2\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{0+0+2x^2}{x.\sqrt{4x^2+x^2+x^2+0}}{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{2x}{\sqrt6\;x}{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex} ...(ii)​​​​​​​
So, from eqn (i) and (ii) we can say given vector is equally inclined with a and c and angle between them is {tex}\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex}

 3
10.(i)

If a line makes 60o and 45o angles with the positive directions of x-axis and z-axis respectively, then find the angle that it makes with the positive direction of y-axis. Hence, write the direction cosines of the line.

Solution:

let {tex}\alpha=\;60^\circ,\beta=?,\gamma=45^\circ{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\cos²\alpha+\cos²\beta+\cos²\gamma=1{/tex}
{tex}\cos^260^\circ+\cos^2\beta+\cos^245^\circ=1{/tex}
{tex}\frac14+\cos^2\beta+\frac12=1{/tex}
{tex}\cos^2\beta=1-\frac34=\frac14{/tex}
{tex}\cos\beta=\frac12{/tex}
{tex}\beta=60^\circ{/tex}
The angle made with y-axis is 60°
Direction cosines = {tex}\left(\frac12,\frac12,\frac1{\sqrt2}\right){/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Check whether the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}{/tex} = z are skew or not.

Solution:

Let {tex}\frac{x-1}2=\frac{y-2}3=\frac{z-3}4=k{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x-4}5=\frac{y-1}2=z{/tex} ...(i)
x = 2k + 1, y = 3k + 2, z = 4k + 3
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}\frac{2k-3}5=\frac{3k+1}2=4k+3{/tex}
On compairing {tex}\frac{2k-3}5=\frac{3k+1}2{/tex}
k = -1
And {tex}\frac{3k+1}2=4k+3{/tex}
k = -1
Therefore the lines are not skew.

 3

SECTION C

Questions number 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the equations of the planes passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} = 6 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0, which are at a distance of 1 unit from the origin.

Solution:

Let the equations of required planes are: {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+3 \hat{j})-6+\lambda[\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-4 \hat{k})]{/tex} = 0
or {tex}\vec{r} \cdot[(1+3 \lambda) \hat{i}+(3-\lambda) \hat{j}-4 \lambda \hat{k}]{/tex}
According to given condition
{tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{(1+3 \lambda)^2+(3-\lambda)^2+(-4 \lambda)^2}}=1{/tex}
36 = {tex}1+9 \lambda^2+6\lambda+9+\lambda^2-6 \lambda+16 \lambda^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 26 \lambda^2=26 \Rightarrow \lambda= \pm 1{/tex}
Equations of planes are {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(4 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k})=6{/tex}, {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})=6{/tex}

 4
12.(i)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}{/tex} = 0, given that y(1) = 0.

Solution:

{tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}=0{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+y=-\frac1{1+x^2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\left(\frac1x\right)y=-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex}
So it a linear differential equation
P = {tex}\frac1x{/tex} and Q = {tex}-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}IF=e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int{\textstyle\frac1x}dx}=e^{\log x}=\;x{/tex}
General solution
{tex}y.IF=\int Q.IFdx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}.x\;dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{\left(1+x^2\right)}\;dx+C{/tex}
{tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+C{/tex} ...(i)
At x =1, y = 0
{tex}0=-\tan^{-1}\left(1\right)+C{/tex}
{tex}C=\frac\pi4{/tex}
Putting in eqn (i)
{tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+\frac\pi4{/tex}

 4

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation x (y3 + x3) dy = (2y4 + 5x3y) dx.

Solution:

x(y3 + x3) dy = (2y4 + 5x3y) dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{2y^4+5x^3y}{xy^3+x^4}{/tex}
It is a homogeneous differential equation
So put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}-v{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v-v^4-v}{v^3+1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{v^3+1}{v^4+4v}dv=\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{4v^3+4}{v^4+4v}dv=4\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
log|v4 + 4v| = 4log x + log C
log|v4 + 4v| = log(x)4 + log C
log|v4 + 4v| = log Cx4
{tex}{/tex}v4 + 4v = Cx4
Put v = {tex}\frac yx{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y^4}{x^4}+4\frac yx{/tex} = Cx4
y4 + 4yx3 = Cx8 

 4
13.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(2 \log \cos x-\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log 2 \sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} {{tex}\because sin2x=2sin x\ cos x{/tex}}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} 2 \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x {/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \sin x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} {{tex}\because{/tex} log(ab) = loga - logb}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log [\sin (\pi / 2-x)] d x{/tex} {{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(x){/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(a-x) d x{/tex}}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex}
= - {tex}\log2\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2[x]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex}
= - {tex}\log 2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-0\right){/tex} = - {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2{/tex}

 4

Case-Study Based Question
14.

In a game of Archery, each ring of the Archery target is valued. The centremost ring is worth 10 points and rest of the rings are allotted points 9 to 1 in sequential order moving outwards.
Archer A is likely to earn 10 points with a probability of 0.8 and Archer B is likely the earn 10 points with a probability of 0.9.

If both of them hit the Archery target, then find the probability that

 4
(i)

exactly one of them earns 10 points.

Solution:

Assume, that
P(A) = probability of Archer A is likely to earn 10 points = 0.8
P(B) = probability of Archer B is likely to earn 10 points = 0.9
Now, for P ({tex}\bar A{/tex}) = 1 - P(A) = 1 - 0.8 = 0.2 and P ({tex}\bar B{/tex}) = 1 - P(B) = 1 - 0.9 = 0.1
Now, the required probability for exactly one of them earns 10 points
= P(A){tex}\cdot{/tex}P({tex}\bar B{/tex}) + P({tex}\bar A{/tex}){tex}\cdot{/tex}P(B)
= 0.8{tex}\cdot{/tex}0.1 + 0.2{tex}\cdot{/tex}0.9
= 0.08 + 0.18
= 0.26

 2
(ii)

both of them earn 10 points.

Solution:

Assume, that
P(A) = probability of Archer A is likely to earn 10 points = 0.8
P(B) = probability of Archer B is likely to earn 10 points = 0.9
Now, for P({tex}\bar A{/tex}) = 1 - P(A) = 1 - 0.8 = 0.2 and P({tex}\bar B{/tex}) = 1 - P(B) = 1 - 0.9 = 0.1
Now, the required probability for both of them earn 10 points,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B) = P(A) {tex}\times{/tex} P(B) = 0.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.9 = 0.72
Therefore, Required probability is 0.26

 2
answer_key_text: Annual Exam - 2022 Code: 65/4/1 (set-1) Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate. Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it. 15 minutes time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period. MATHEMATICS Time allowed:2 hours Maximum Marks:40 General Instructions : Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them : This question paper contains three sections - Section A , B and C . Each section is compulsory . Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each. There is an internal choice in some questions. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each. SECTION A Questions number 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each. 1. A bag contains 3 red and 4 white balls. Three balls are drawn at random, one-by-one without replacement from the bag. If the first ball drawn is red in colour, then find the probability that the remaining two balls drawn are also red in colour. Solution: Total balls = 3 + 4 = 7 Now if 1st ball was red then red balls left = 2 Total balls = 6 P(B 2 ) = {tex}\frac{{}^2C_1}{{}^6C_1}{/tex} ​​ ​​​​​And now for 3rd ball, red ball left = 1 Total balls= 5 P(B 3 ) = {tex}\frac{{}^1C_1}{{}^5C_1}{/tex} Now, required probability= {tex}\frac{{}^2C_1}{{}^6C_1}\times\frac{{}^1C_1}{{}^5C_1}=\frac26\times\frac15=\frac1{15}{/tex} 2 2. A coin is tossed twice. The following table shows the probability distribution of the number of tails: X 0 1 2 P(X) K 6K 9K Find the value of K. Is the coin tossed biased or unbiased? Justify your answer. Solution: We know that, the Total probability should be equal to 1 for sum of all possible events under the given experiment. Therefore, P(0) + P(1) + P(2) = 1 K + 6K + 9K = 1 (From the table) 16K = 1 {tex}\because{/tex} K = {tex}\frac 1{16}{/tex} From results we can see that events are not equally likely, hence the coin tossed is unbiased, Because P(0) {tex}\ne{/tex} P(2) {tex}\because{/tex} K {tex}\ne{/tex} 9K. 2 3. The foot of a perpendicular drawn from the point (-2, -1, -3) on a plane is (1, -3, 3). Find the equation of the plane. Solution: We observe that the required plane passes through the point Q (1, - 3, 3) and is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow { P Q } = ( \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ) - ( - 2 \hat { i } - {/tex} {tex}\hat { j } - 3 \hat { k } ) = 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k }{/tex} . Thus, the required plane passes through the point Q having a position vector {tex}\vec { a } = \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} and is normal to {tex}\vec { n } = \overrightarrow { P Q } = 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k }{/tex} . So, the vector equation of the plane is {tex}\vec { r } \cdot \vec { n } = \vec { a } \cdot \vec { n }{/tex} or, {tex}\vec { r } \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ) = ( \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex} or, {tex}\vec { r } \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex} = 3 + 6 + 18 or, {tex}\vec { r } \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex} = 27 The cartesian equation of the plane is {tex}( x \hat { i } + y \hat { j } + z \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 3 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + 6 \hat { k } ){/tex} = 27 or, 3x - 2y + 6z = 27 2 4. (i) If {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } {/tex} = 400 and {tex} | \vec { b } |{/tex} = 5, then find the value of {tex} | \vec { a } |{/tex} . Solution: Given, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} and {tex} | \vec { a } | = 5{/tex} Consider, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 400{/tex} {tex} [ \because | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \text { and } | \vec { a } . \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \cos \theta \|{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow d | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \left( \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta \right) = 400{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 25 | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400 \quad [ \because | \vec { a } | = 5 ]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | ^{ 2 } = 16 \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | = 4{/tex} [ {tex} \therefore{/tex} length cannot be negative] 2 OR (ii) Find all the possible vectors of magnitude {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} which are equally inclined to the coordinate axes. Solution: Direction vector equally inclined to coordinate axis = {tex}\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex} Vector = {tex}5\sqrt3\times\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex} Vector = {tex}5\widehat i+5\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex} 2 5. Find the general solution of the differential equation sec 2 x tan y dx + sec 2 y tan x dy = 0 Solution: Given Differential equation is, sec 2 x tan y dx + sec 2 y tan x dy = 0 Dividing by tan x tan y, we have {tex}\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow\int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}x}}{{\tan x}}dx + \int\frac{{{{\sec }^2}y}}{{\tan y}}dy = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x} \right| + \log \left| {\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \int {\frac{{f'\left( x \right)}}{{f\left( x \right)}}} dx = \log \left| {f\left( x \right)} \right|} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \log \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = \log c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left| {\tan x\tan y} \right| = c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \tan x\tan y = \pm c = C{/tex} ... {tex}\left[ {\because \,\left| t \right| = a\left( {a \geqslant 0} \right) \Rightarrow t = \pm a} \right]{/tex} 2 6. Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^1 x^2 e^x d x{/tex} Solution: {tex}\int_0^1 x^2 e^x d x=\left[x^2 \cdot e^x\right]_0^1-\int_0^1 2 x \ e^x d x{/tex} {tex}=\left(1 \cdot e^1-0\right)-2 \int_0^1 x \ e^x dx{/tex} = {tex}e-2\left(\left[x \ e^x\right]_0^1-\int_0^1 1 . e^x d x\right){/tex} = {tex}e-2\left(1 . e^1-0\right)+2 \int_0^1 e^x d x{/tex} = {tex}e-2 e+2\left[e^x\right]_0^1{/tex} = -e + 2(e 1 - e 0 ) = -e + 2 (e - 1) = e - 2 2 SECTION B Questions number 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each. 7. Find the area of the region {(x, y) : {tex}\left.x^2 \leq y \leq x+2\right\}{/tex} , using integration. Solution: {tex}\because{/tex} y = x + 2 and y = x 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x 2 = x + 2 x 2 - x - 2 = 0 x 2 - 2x + x - 2 = 0 x(x - 2) + 1(x - 2) = 0 (x - 2)(x + 1) = 0 x = 2 and -1 y = 2 + 2 = 4 and y = -1 + 2 = 1 So C(-1, 1) and D (2, 4) Now required area = area covered by line - area of parabola Required area = {tex}\int_\limits{-1}^2(x+2)dx-\int_\limits{-1}^2x^2dx{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{x^2}2+2x\right]_{-1}^2-\left[\frac{x^3}3\right]_{-1}^2{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac42+4-\frac12+2\right]-\left[\frac83-\frac{\left(-1\right)}3\right]{/tex} Required area = {tex}\frac{15}2-3=\frac92{/tex} sq. unit. 3 8. (i) Find: {tex}\int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex} Solution: Let {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } + 1 } d x{/tex} {tex}= \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } } \right] } d x{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \left[ 1 + e ^ { - x } \right] } d x{/tex} ...(i) Let 1 + e -x = t then, d(1 + e -x ) = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - e -x dx = dt {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = {tex}\frac { - d t } { e ^ { - x } }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} dx = - e x dt Putting 1 + e -x = t and dx = - e x dt in equation (i), we get, {tex}I = \int \frac { 1 } { e ^ { x } \times t } \times - e ^ { x } d t{/tex} {tex}= - \int \frac { d t } { t }{/tex} = - log |t| + c = - log |1 + e -x | + c = - log |1 + e -x | + c 3 OR (ii) Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex} Solution: {tex}I=\int \limits_1^4\{|x|+|3-x|\} d x{/tex} ​​​​​ |3 - x| = -(3 - x) for 1 and 2 |3 - x| = (3 - x) for 3 and 4 So, {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx+\int_1^2-(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\int\limits_1^4xdx-\int_1^2(3-x)dx+\int_3^4(3-x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=\left[\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^4-\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_1^2+\left[3x-\frac{x^2}2\right]_3^4{/tex} {tex}I=\left[{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-{\textstyle\frac12}\right]-\left[6-{\textstyle\frac42}-3+{\textstyle\frac12}\right]+{/tex} {tex}\left[12-{\textstyle\frac{16}2}-9+{\textstyle\frac92}\right]{/tex} I = {tex}\frac{11}2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 3 9. If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitude, then prove that the vector {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} is equally inclined to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} . Also, find the angle between {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} . Solution: Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=x{/tex} ​​Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow aand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} be {tex}\theta_1{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{\overrightarrow a.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex} ​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\overrightarrow a\overrightarrow{.b}+2\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x^2+0+0}{x.\sqrt{4x^2+x^2+x^2+0}}{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x}{\sqrt6\;x}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex} ...(i)​​​​​​​ Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow cand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} be ​​​​​​​ {tex}\theta_2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{\overrightarrow c.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{2\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow c\overrightarrow{.b}+2\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{0+0+2x^2}{x.\sqrt{4x^2+x^2+x^2+0}}{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{2x}{\sqrt6\;x}{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex} ...(ii)​​​​​​​ So, from eqn (i) and (ii) we can say given vector is equally inclined with a and c and angle between them is {tex}\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex} 3 10. (i) If a line makes 60 o and 45 o angles with the positive directions of x-axis and z-axis respectively, then find the angle that it makes with the positive direction of y-axis. Hence, write the direction cosines of the line. Solution: let {tex}\alpha=\;60^\circ,\beta=?,\gamma=45^\circ{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\cos²\alpha+\cos²\beta+\cos²\gamma=1{/tex} {tex}\cos^260^\circ+\cos^2\beta+\cos^245^\circ=1{/tex} {tex}\frac14+\cos^2\beta+\frac12=1{/tex} {tex}\cos^2\beta=1-\frac34=\frac14{/tex} {tex}\cos\beta=\frac12{/tex} {tex}\beta=60^\circ{/tex} The angle made with y-axis is 60° Direction cosines = {tex}\left(\frac12,\frac12,\frac1{\sqrt2}\right){/tex} 3 OR (ii) Check whether the lines {tex}\frac{x-1}{2}=\frac{y-2}{3}=\frac{z-3}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x-4}{5}=\frac{y-1}{2}{/tex} = z are skew or not. Solution: Let {tex}\frac{x-1}2=\frac{y-2}3=\frac{z-3}4=k{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-4}5=\frac{y-1}2=z{/tex} ...(i) x = 2k + 1, y = 3k + 2, z = 4k + 3 Putting in eqn (i) {tex}\frac{2k-3}5=\frac{3k+1}2=4k+3{/tex} On compairing {tex}\frac{2k-3}5=\frac{3k+1}2{/tex} k = -1 And {tex}\frac{3k+1}2=4k+3{/tex} k = -1 Therefore the lines are not skew. 3 SECTION C Questions number 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each. 11. Find the equations of the planes passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} = 6 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0, which are at a distance of 1 unit from the origin. Solution: Let the equations of required planes are: {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+3 \hat{j})-6+\lambda[\vec{r} \cdot(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-4 \hat{k})]{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{r} \cdot[(1+3 \lambda) \hat{i}+(3-\lambda) \hat{j}-4 \lambda \hat{k}]{/tex} According to given condition {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{(1+3 \lambda)^2+(3-\lambda)^2+(-4 \lambda)^2}}=1{/tex} 36 = {tex}1+9 \lambda^2+6\lambda+9+\lambda^2-6 \lambda+16 \lambda^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 26 \lambda^2=26 \Rightarrow \lambda= \pm 1{/tex} Equations of planes are {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(4 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-4 \hat{k})=6{/tex} , {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})=6{/tex} 4 12. (i) Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}{/tex} = 0, given that y(1) = 0. Solution: {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x}+y+\frac{1}{1+x^2}=0{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dy}{dx}+y=-\frac1{1+x^2}{/tex} {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}+\left(\frac1x\right)y=-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex} So it a linear differential equation P = {tex}\frac1x{/tex} and Q = {tex}-\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}{/tex} {tex}IF=e^{\int pdx}=e^{\int{\textstyle\frac1x}dx}=e^{\log x}=\;x{/tex} General solution {tex}y.IF=\int Q.IFdx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{x\left(1+x^2\right)}.x\;dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\int\frac1{\left(1+x^2\right)}\;dx+C{/tex} {tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+C{/tex} ...(i) At x =1, y = 0 {tex}0=-\tan^{-1}\left(1\right)+C{/tex} {tex}C=\frac\pi4{/tex} Putting in eqn (i) {tex}y.x=-\tan^{-1}x+\frac\pi4{/tex} 4 OR (ii) Find the general solution of the differential equation x (y 3 + x 3 ) dy = (2y 4 + 5x 3 y) dx. Solution: x(y 3 + x 3 ) dy = (2y 4 + 5x 3 y) dx {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{2y^4+5x^3y}{xy^3+x^4}{/tex} It is a homogeneous differential equation So put y = vx, {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex} {tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v}{v^3+1}-v{/tex} {tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{2v^4+5v-v^4-v}{v^3+1}{/tex} {tex}\frac{v^3+1}{v^4+4v}dv=\frac{dx}x{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{4v^3+4}{v^4+4v}dv=4\int\frac{dx}x{/tex} log|v 4 + 4v| = 4log x + log C log|v 4 + 4v| = log(x) 4 + log C log|v 4 + 4v| = log Cx 4 {tex}{/tex} v 4 + 4v = Cx 4 Put v = {tex}\frac yx{/tex} {tex}\frac{y^4}{x^4}+4\frac yx{/tex} = Cx 4 y 4 + 4yx 3 = Cx 8 4 13. Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}(2 \log \cos x-\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex} Solution: {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log \sin 2 x) d x{/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2}(2 \log \cos x{/tex} - {tex}\log 2 \sin x \cos x) d x{/tex} { {tex}\because sin2x=2sin x\ cos x{/tex} } = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} 2 \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x {/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \sin x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} log(ab) = loga - logb} = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log [\sin (\pi / 2-x)] d x{/tex} { {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(x){/tex} = {tex}\int_\limits0^a f(a-x) d x{/tex} } = {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log 2 d x{/tex} - {tex}\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} \log \cos x d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log2\int_\limits0^{\pi / 2} d x{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2[x]_0^{\pi / 2}{/tex} = - {tex}\log 2\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-0\right){/tex} = - {tex}\frac{\pi}{2} \log 2{/tex} 4 Case-Study Based Question 14. In a game of Archery, each ring of the Archery target is valued. The centremost ring is worth 10 points and rest of the rings are allotted points 9 to 1 in sequential order moving outwards. Archer A is likely to earn 10 points with a probability of 0.8 and Archer B is likely the earn 10 points with a probability of 0.9. If both of them hit the Archery target, then find the probability that 4 (i) exactly one of them earns 10 points. Solution: Assume, that P(A) = probability of Archer A is likely to earn 10 points = 0.8 P(B) = probability of Archer B is likely to earn 10 points = 0.9 Now, for P ( {tex}\bar A{/tex} ) = 1 - P(A) = 1 - 0.8 = 0.2 and P ( {tex}\bar B{/tex} ) = 1 - P(B) = 1 - 0.9 = 0.1 Now, the required probability for exactly one of them earns 10 points = P(A) {tex}\cdot{/tex} P( {tex}\bar B{/tex} ) + P( {tex}\bar A{/tex} ) {tex}\cdot{/tex} P(B) = 0.8 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 0.1 + 0.2 {tex}\cdot{/tex} 0.9 = 0.08 + 0.18 = 0.26 2 (ii) both of them earn 10 points. Solution: Assume, that P(A) = probability of Archer A is likely to earn 10 points = 0.8 P(B) = probability of Archer B is likely to earn 10 points = 0.9 Now, for P( {tex}\bar A{/tex} ) = 1 - P(A) = 1 - 0.8 = 0.2 and P( {tex}\bar B{/tex} ) = 1 - P(B) = 1 - 0.9 = 0.1 Now, the required probability for both of them earn 10 points, {tex}\therefore{/tex} P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} B) = P(A) {tex}\times{/tex} P(B) = 0.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.9 = 0.72 Therefore, Required probability is 0.26 2
